reasoning: The Lean signature encodes the informal claim exactly: `[Fintype G]` with `card G = 105` (via `open Fintype`) captures "group of order 105"; `P : Sylow 3 G` with `[hP : P.Normal]` captures "a Sylow 3-subgroup is normal" (verified against `Mathlib/GroupTheory/Sylow.lean` where `P.Normal` refers to the underlying subgroup being normal); and `∀ a b : G, a*b = b*a` is the standard definition of G being abelian. Though the Lean form universally quantifies over P while the informal says "a Sylow 3-subgroup", these are equivalent because Sylow 3-subgroups always exist (3 ∣ 105) and all are conjugate, so one being normal is equivalent to every one being normal. No extra or missing hypotheses beyond what the informal statement asserts.
